text: The program is taught by leading faculty.
Applicants are evaluated on their leadership record.
Cases are discussed in small groups.
The capstone is presented at the end of the program.
Hi Eliane, thanks for your time. I direct admissions here, and I would love to tell you how the program is run.
Thank you, David. I am a physician opening a fertility clinic. Could you tell me how the curriculum is taught?
Most of it is taught through the case method. Real cases are discussed by the whole cohort, and every voice is expected to be heard.
That is helpful. And how are admissions decided? I want to know how applicants are evaluated.
Good to hear. Is the faculty drawn only from the business school, or from medicine too?
Both. The program is co-taught, and the opening keynote is often given by a physician-entrepreneur. Our accreditation is international, too.
One last question. How is the final capstone evaluated?
It is presented to the whole cohort and reviewed by a faculty panel. Yours could be your clinic. I hope your application is submitted soon.
May I build on that point?
In my experience as a physician, the opposite is often true.
Could I be introduced to the faculty in reproductive medicine?
I would love to stay in touch after the program.

Hi Eliane, and welcome. I am David, and I direct admissions for our executive-education program. Let me explain how the program is run, so you know what to expect. Applicants are evaluated on their leadership record, not only on test scores, so your work as a physician and a founder is a real strength. The curriculum is taught by leading faculty from the medical school and the business school together. Most of the learning is done through the case method: real cases are discussed in small groups, and every voice is expected to be heard. You are placed in a cohort of about sixty peers from all over the world, and lifelong connections are formed there. At the end, a capstone project is presented to the whole cohort. Do not be shy. In this room, quiet excellence is not enough; your ideas have to be spoken.

Hi Eliane, it is Sofia. We were placed in the same cohort last year, and you asked me what the experience is really like, so here are a few honest thoughts. The immersion is intense. Every case is prepared the night before and then debated the next morning, and at first I was terrified to speak. What helped me was a simple habit: I decided that one comment had to be made by me in every single session. Within two weeks, my hand was raised without fear. The keynote speakers are extraordinary, but the real value is the network. Coffee breaks are where the deals are made, honestly. My advice is this: your accreditation and your title open the door, but your voice is what makes people remember you. You will hold your own beautifully. Call me anytime.

Applicants are evaluated on their leadership record.
Cases are discussed in small groups.
The capstone is presented at the end of the program.
The case was written by the team.
The keynote was given by an alumna.
